explanationZhTw: 這裡的「the lonely hour of fifteen」是帶有文學色彩的說法，指的是下午三點左右；不是字面上的「孤獨時刻」，而是刻意營造寂靜、空曠的氛圍。
generatedExample: At the lonely hour of five, the office felt strangely empty.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreens」是此作品中的特定名詞，指具有電視與監視功能的螢幕裝置；在一般英語裡不是常見日常詞。
generatedExample: The telescreens in the hallway never seemed to switch off.

explanationZhTw: 「gazing」指「凝視、目不轉睛地看」，比一般的「看」更強調長時間、專注地注視。
generatedExample: She was gazing out the window and did not hear me come in.

explanationZhTw: 「glanced up」是片語動詞，意思是「抬頭看了一眼」。
generatedExample: He glanced up from his book when the phone rang.

explanationZhTw: 「vast」表示「巨大的、廣大的」。這裡用來形容臉龐，帶有壓迫感。
generatedExample: They crossed a vast open field before sunset.

explanationZhTw: 「eyed」在這裡不是名詞「眼睛」，而是動詞，意思是「盯著看、打量」。
generatedExample: The cat eyed the fish on the table.

explanationZhTw: 「caption」是「圖說、標題文字；附註文字」。這裡指牆上的標語文字。
generatedExample: The photo caption explained where the scene was taken.

explanationZhTw: 「Unbidden」表示「未經要求地、主動地、自己就來了」。
generatedExample: Unbidden, she began to help us with the cleanup.

explanationZhTw: 「filled ... up」是片語動詞，意思是「把……裝滿」。
generatedExample: Please fill the bottle up with water before you leave.

explanationZhTw: 「Victory Gin」是專有名詞，指小說裡的酒名；在這裡是帶有時代背景意味的飲品名稱。
generatedExample: He ordered a glass of Victory Gin before dinner.

explanationZhTw: 「saccharine」表示「過甜的；甜得膩人」，也可引申為「矯情做作的」。
generatedExample: The tea was so saccharine that I could hardly drink it.

explanationZhTw: 「speciality」表示「招牌菜／特色品、擅長之處」。這裡指咖啡館的特色飲品。
generatedExample: Seafood is the restaurant's speciality.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的虛構裝置，兼具廣播與監視功能；這裡是專有名詞式的科幻/反烏托邦詞彙。
generatedExample: The telescreen blinked to life before dawn.

explanationZhTw: 指官方發布的簡短緊急新聞或公告；這裡是政府/軍事語境中的正式用法。
generatedExample: The radio station interrupted the show with a special bulletin.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「前面」，而是「戰線、前線」；屬於軍事語境中的特殊意思。
generatedExample: The commander said the front had moved north overnight.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「令人不安的、使人焦慮的」；比一般的 worried 更正式、更強烈。
generatedExample: Her silence was disquieting to everyone in the room.

explanationZhTw: 表示「斷斷續續、時有時無」；常用來描述動作或狀態不是連續發生。
generatedExample: It rained on and off throughout the afternoon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「受到威脅」的正式說法，不只是一般的「被害怕」，而是有外在危險逼近。
generatedExample: The town felt menaced by the spreading fire.

explanationZhTw: 此處指情緒「突然湧起、變得強烈」；不是字面上的火焰，而是說感受一下子爆發。
generatedExample: Anger flared up when he heard the accusation.

explanationZhTw: 指「一大口喝乾」；at a gulp 表示用很大的吞嚥動作快速喝完。
generatedExample: She drained the juice at a gulp before running out the door.

explanationZhTw: 指「作嘔、乾嘔、想吐」；通常是因為氣味或味道太難受。
generatedExample: The rotten smell made him retch.

explanationZhTw: 口語中指「那玩意兒、那東西」；這裡是指前文提到的杜松子酒。
generatedExample: What is this stuff on my desk?

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「生病的」，而是指味道或感覺「令人不舒服、過甜膩人、噁心」。
generatedExample: The drink had a sickly sweet taste.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「掩蓋、遮掩」的意思，表示香料的味道無法把原本的臭味蓋掉。
generatedExample: The perfume could not disguise the smell of smoke.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `visualized` 不是單純「看見」，而是「在腦中形成影像、想像出來」。
generatedExample: I can describe the room, but I still can't visualize it clearly.

explanationZhTw: `half-aware of` 表示「只隱約察覺到、意識到一部分」，不是完全沒注意，也不是完全清楚。
generatedExample: I was half-aware of music playing somewhere down the hall.

explanationZhTw: `hovering` 在這裡不是「盤旋飛行」，而是用來形容氣味或感覺「縈繞在附近、久久不散」。
generatedExample: The smell of smoke was hovering close to the doorway.

explanationZhTw: `clung to` 這裡表示氣味「緊黏著、揮之不去」，不是字面上的抓住。
generatedExample: The smell of garlic clung to my hands after dinner.

explanationZhTw: `unbidden` 表示「未經要求就主動出現／送來」，帶有「不必開口就來了」的意思。
generatedExample: Unbidden, she started cleaning up the table before anyone asked.

explanationZhTw: `sinecure` 指「閒職、輕鬆但薪水不錯的職位」，重點是工作責任很少。
generatedExample: After years of hard work, he was shocked to be offered a sinecure.

explanationZhTw: 小說中的專有名詞，指一種結合螢幕、廣播與監視功能的裝置，不是一般的電視。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the corner never stayed off for long.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「接手、接替」；聲音開始主持播送。
generatedExample: When the host got sick, a guest speaker took over.

explanationZhTw: 指簡短的新聞或官方通報，這裡是前線戰況簡報。
generatedExample: The office posted bulletins on the door every morning.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「前面」，而是「前線、戰線」。
generatedExample: The soldiers waited for news from the front.

explanationZhTw: 指正式的公告、通告或宣告。
generatedExample: They made an announcement before the meeting started.

explanationZhTw: 表示「先前的、前一個的」。
generatedExample: Please review the preceding chapter before continuing.

explanationZhTw: 指分配額、配額；這裡是要求完成的產量目標。
generatedExample: The factory met its quota by Friday afternoon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「超額完成、超過目標」；是帶有官僚語氣的說法。
generatedExample: The team overfulfilled its sales target this month.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「擺放、排列好」棋子，不是「出發」。
generatedExample: She set out the documents in neat piles before the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「棘手的、難處理的」，用來形容這個棋局收尾不容易。
generatedExample: The puzzle looked simple, but the last step was tricky.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 couple of 表示「兩個」，整句是在說有兩個馬（棋子）參與這個局面。
generatedExample: The teacher gave us a couple of examples to follow.

explanationZhTw: 棋類術語，意思是「兩步將死對方」，mate 在這裡不是「同伴」，而是「將死」。
generatedExample: He found a mate in two moves and won the game.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「抬頭看」。
generatedExample: When I heard my name, I looked up from my notes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指天氣，而是指想法模糊、帶點神祕感的信念。
generatedExample: His answer was wrapped in cloudy mysticism instead of clear facts.

explanationZhTw: 固定說法，意思是「毫無例外、全部都是這樣」。
generatedExample: Everyone in the class, without exception, completed the assignment.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 arranged 不是單純「整理好」，而是表示「被如此設定／安排成這樣」。
generatedExample: The schedule is arranged so that each team gets equal time.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「象徵、代表」，用來說棋局反映了更大的概念。
generatedExample: The red rose can symbolize love or respect.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「看」，而是帶著持續、凝視的感覺「回望」過來。
generatedExample: She gazed back at the painting as if it were alive.

explanationZhTw: 這是《1984》中的特定名詞，指兼具電視播放與監視功能的裝置，不是一般日常說的電視螢幕。
generatedExample: The telescreen crackled to life and filled the room with a government message.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 graver 是「更嚴肅、更莊重」的意思，不是字面上的「墳墓」相關意思。
generatedExample: Her graver tone told everyone the situation was serious.

explanationZhTw: `stand by` 這裡表示「待命、準備好、等候通知」，不是單純的「站在旁邊」。
generatedExample: Please stand by for further instructions.

explanationZhTw: `announcement` 是「公告、正式通知」；在這裡指即將發布的重要消息。
generatedExample: The school made an announcement about the schedule change.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是提醒語，意思是「小心、注意」，帶有警告語氣。
generatedExample: Take care when you cross the busy street.

explanationZhTw: `strike up` 在這裡表示「開始演奏、響起」，常用來說音樂或對話開始。
generatedExample: A cheerful tune struck up as the parade began.

explanationZhTw: `bulletin` 在這裡是「公報、簡報式的消息」，特別像戰時傳來的官方消息，不是一般隨口聽到的新聞。
generatedExample: The radio bulletin warned everyone to stay indoors.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `front` 不是「前面」，而是軍事語境中的「前線、戰線」。
generatedExample: The commander sent more supplies to the front.

explanationZhTw: `instinct` 是「直覺、本能」；這裡指他還沒看到內容，就憑感覺知道情況不好。
generatedExample: My instinct told me not to trust that deal.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，表示某個念頭「反覆浮現又消失」，不是字面上的進進出出。
generatedExample: The question kept going in and out of her mind all night.

explanationZhTw: `stood out` 在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「格外清楚地顯現、突出」，不是單純站出來。
generatedExample: Her name stood out on the list because it was written in red.

explanationZhTw: `proper spot` 在這裡指「最合適、最正確的位置」，帶有很強的判斷感。
generatedExample: This corner is the proper spot for the lamp.

explanationZhTw: `horde` 指「一大群人或軍隊」，常帶有混亂、壓迫或貶義色彩；這裡強化敵軍大舉壓境的畫面。
generatedExample: A horde of fans gathered outside the stadium.

explanationZhTw: `rear` 在軍事語境中是「後方、後面」，不是一般最常見的「養育」或其他用法。
generatedExample: The scouts moved behind the enemy and attacked from the rear.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `communications` 指軍事上的「聯絡線、交通與補給通路」，不是單純的通訊設備。
generatedExample: The bridge was destroyed, cutting the village's communications.

explanationZhTw: `medley` 是「混合物、雜糅的一團」；這裡比喻多種情緒混在一起，難以分開。
generatedExample: The album is a medley of jazz, rock, and folk styles.

explanationZhTw: `medley` 是「混合物、雜糅的一團」；這裡比喻多種情緒混在一起，難以分開。
generatedExample: The album is a medley of jazz, rock, and folk styles.

explanationZhTw: 「spasm」指痙攣、抽搐；這裡是身體突然一陣不自主的緊縮或抽動。
generatedExample: A spasm in his leg woke him up at midnight.

explanationZhTw: 「for the moment」表示暫時、眼下先這樣，暗示之後可能會改變。
generatedExample: For the moment, we can leave the door open.

explanationZhTw: 「settle down」是片語動詞，這裡指安定下來、靜下心來開始專心做事。
generatedExample: It took me ten minutes to settle down and start reading.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「wandered」不是指真的走來走去，而是指思緒飄走、走神。
generatedExample: During the lecture, my thoughts wandered to dinner.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「traced」是用手指在灰塵上畫出痕跡，屬於「描畫、劃出」的意思。
generatedExample: She traced a circle on the window with her finger.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的數學等式，而是象徵權力強迫人們接受明知錯誤的說法，代表對現實的扭曲與精神控制。
generatedExample: In the meeting, they acted like 2+2=5 and ignored the obvious accounting error.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「進到身體裡」，而是指心理上滲入、控制或深深影響一個人。
generatedExample: The criticism really got inside her and ruined her confidence.

explanationZhTw: 表示「永遠、永久地」；在這裡強調某種影響是不可逆的，會一直留著。
generatedExample: She said the mistake would stay with him for ever.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指從精神、情感或創傷中復原，不只是身體康復。
generatedExample: It took him years to recover from the loss.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「燒毀殆盡」；此處用來強調內在被徹底破壞、消滅。
generatedExample: The old hut burnt out after the lightning strike.

explanationZhTw: cauterize 原本是用灼燒止血或封住傷口；這裡是比喻性的說法，表示那部分感受被灼燒到連痕跡都消失。
generatedExample: The shock seemed to cauterized out every trace of hope.

explanationZhTw: 表示「偶然地、碰巧」；不是事先安排好的，而是剛好遇到。
generatedExample: We met by chance at the station and had coffee together.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「對某人有任何注意／關心」；這裡表示沒有人會在意他。
generatedExample: The teacher did not take any interest in the argument.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是副詞，表示「斜斜地、側著地」；不是直接朝前走，而是偏斜的方向。
generatedExample: The path ran obliquely across the field.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，表示「擺脫、趕走、除去」；這裡是想把他甩開。
generatedExample: I need to get rid of these old boxes before the move.

explanationZhTw: 表示「勉強接受、只好認命接受」；通常是對不太喜歡的情況不再反抗。
generatedExample: After arguing for hours, she resigned herself to the delay.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，表示「隱藏、遮蔽」；這裡指這些灌木無法拿來躲藏。
generatedExample: The cave offered good concealment from view.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的虛構監視裝置，兼具螢幕與監聽功能；這裡不是一般的「電視螢幕」。
generatedExample: Every apartment had a telescreen above the door.

explanationZhTw: matter 在這裡不是「有物質」的意思，而是「重要、要緊」；整句表示「一點也不重要」。
generatedExample: It didn't matter whether we came early or late.

explanationZhTw: lie down 的過去分詞，意思是「躺下」；這裡是動作本身，不是比喻。
generatedExample: He had lain down for a minute before the phone rang.

explanationZhTw: 這是強烈的比喻說法，表示因恐懼而全身發冷、僵住。
generatedExample: Her flesh froze with horror when she heard the footsteps behind her.

explanationZhTw: disengage herself 是較正式的說法，指把自己從對方的接觸、束縛或情境中抽開／掙脫。
generatedExample: She tried to disengage herself from the crowd and leave quietly.

explanationZhTw: sallow 指膚色蠟黃、沒有血色；這裡形容臉色憔悴、不健康。
generatedExample: After several nights without sleep, his face looked sallower than before.

explanationZhTw: occur to sb. 表示「某人突然想到」；這裡指他忽然冒出一個想法。
generatedExample: It occurred to him that he had left the keys in the car.

explanationZhTw: 短暫的一瞥；momentary 表示「瞬間的」，glance 是「快速看一眼／一瞥」。
generatedExample: She gave him a momentary glance before looking away.

explanationZhTw: 輕蔑、鄙視；這比一般的 dislike 更強烈，帶有看不起對方的意味。
generatedExample: His voice was full of contempt when he answered.

explanationZhTw: 浮腫的臉、腫起來的臉；這裡指臉看起來不自然地腫脹。
generatedExample: After the long flight, he woke up with a bloated face.

explanationZhTw: 把眼淚「擠」出來；這裡是比喻說法，指風讓淚水不斷從眼睛流出。
generatedExample: The cold wind was squeezing tears from her eyes.

explanationZhTw: 並排地、肩並肩地。
generatedExample: The two friends walked side by side down the street.

explanationZhTw: 故意地、有意識地；表示動作不是不小心做的。
generatedExample: He deliberately spoke more slowly so everyone could follow.

explanationZhTw: 動詞 `betray` 在這裡是「背叛、出賣」的意思，表示對某人失信或把對方的秘密、立場交出去。
generatedExample: He felt ashamed that he betrayed his friend's trust.

explanationZhTw: `baldly` 這裡不是指「禿頭地」，而是指「直白地、毫不掩飾地、乾脆地」說出來，帶點冷淡或不加修飾的語氣。
generatedExample: She baldly admitted that she had lied.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `betrayed` 是「背叛、出賣」的意思，指對某人做了不忠或違背信任的事。在這句話裡，說話者承認自己傷害了對方的信任。
generatedExample: She felt hurt because her best friend betrayed her trust.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `gave ... a look` 不是「給了某個東西」，而是指「看了他一眼／瞥了他一眼」。`look of dislike` 表示帶有厭惡、不悅的表情或眼神，屬於依上下文理解的用法。
generatedExample: She gave the manager a quick look of irritation before leaving the room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `stand up to` 不是字面上的「站起來面對」，而是指「承受、對抗、挺住」某種壓力或威脅。
generatedExample: I couldn't stand up to the pressure at work, so I asked for help.

explanationZhTw: `pretend` 在這裡是「假裝、裝作」的意思，指事後把自己的真實意思否認掉。
generatedExample: She pretended she was fine, but she was actually very upset.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `save yourself` 指「保住自己、讓自己脫困」，不是一般字面上的「節省自己」。
generatedExample: He lied to save himself from punishment.

explanationZhTw: `don't give a damn` 是粗俗慣用語，表示「一點也不在乎」。
generatedExample: He doesn't give a damn what other people think.

explanationZhTw: 「care about」表示「在意、關心」，這裡是常見片語動詞／動詞片語用法，屬於基礎但很實用的表達。
generatedExample: She doesn't care about winning; she just wants to improve.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「echoed」不是字面上的「回音」，而是指「重複別人剛說的話」，屬於語境中的特殊用法。
generatedExample: After hearing the plan, Tom echoed, 'That won't work.'

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「對某人不再有原本相同的感受／態度」，不是字面上的「感覺一樣朝向某人」。
generatedExample: After the argument, I didn't feel the same towards him.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「不再、再也不」的意思，常用在否定句中，表示某種狀態持續到現在已經結束了。
generatedExample: I don't work there any longer.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `feel the same` 不是單純指「感覺一樣」，而是指「你的感受/想法已經和以前不同了」。
generatedExample: After the trip, I don’t feel the same about the city anymore.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是『貼上』的字面意思，而是指風勢很強，把衣服緊緊壓貼在身上，帶出被風壓住的感覺。
generatedExample: The rain plastered my jacket to my skin.

explanationZhTw: 表示『立刻、馬上』，不是字面上的『一次完成』。
generatedExample: At once, she knew the phone call was bad news.

explanationZhTw: 形容讓人尷尬、不自在的情況。
generatedExample: It was embarrassing to ask the same question twice.

explanationZhTw: 在英國口語中，The Tube 指倫敦地鐵；這裡不是一般的『管子』意思。
generatedExample: I took the Tube to the museum.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是『站起來』的意思，屬於常見片語動詞用法。
generatedExample: He stood up when the meeting ended.

explanationZhTw: 「must」表示「必須、一定要」，是最基本的情態動詞之一；這裡用來表示必要或強烈意願，屬於常見的 A2 詞彙。
generatedExample: We must leave now if we want to catch the train.

explanationZhTw: 「meet again」表示「再見面、再次碰面」。這裡是一般用法，不是片語動詞或慣用語，但在對話中很常見，適合留意其整體搭配。
generatedExample: Let's meet again next week to finish the project.

explanationZhTw: `must` 表示「必須、一定要」，這裡是在表達強烈的必要性或決心，屬於常見基礎用法。
generatedExample: We must leave now if we want to catch the train.

explanationZhTw: 指「猶豫不決地、遲疑地」；這裡描述他跟在後面時沒有明確決斷。
generatedExample: She stood irresolutely at the door before finally knocking.

explanationZhTw: 此處是「甩掉、擺脫某人」的意思，不是字面上的「搖掉」。
generatedExample: I tried to shake him off, but he kept following me.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「並排」的字面義，而是指「跟上她的速度、保持同樣步調」。
generatedExample: He walked faster so he could keep abreast of the group.

explanationZhTw: 指「下定決心」。
generatedExample: After thinking for a while, she made up her mind to leave.

explanationZhTw: 指「拖著走、慢慢跟著」，這裡帶有無精打采地跟在後面的感覺。
generatedExample: He trailed along behind the others, too tired to speak.

explanationZhTw: 指「沒有意義的、徒勞的」。
generatedExample: It was pointless to argue after the decision had already been made.

explanationZhTw: 指「難以忍受的」；這裡強調寒冷和拖延的感受讓他受不了。
generatedExample: The noise from the street became unbearable at night.

explanationZhTw: 指被強烈情緒或慾望「壓倒、淹沒」；不是單純「忙不過來」。
generatedExample: She was overwhelmed by relief when she heard the news.

explanationZhTw: 指對過去感到懷念、帶有回憶感的。
generatedExample: The old song made him feel nostalgic for his school days.

explanationZhTw: 指「敷衍的、沒有全力以赴的嘗試」。
generatedExample: Her half-hearted attempt to apologize did not convince anyone.

explanationZhTw: 指「匆匆離開、溜走」；通常帶有悄悄離開或趕快走掉的語感。
generatedExample: When the alarm sounded, the thief made off through the back door.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「看清楚、分辨出來」的意思，不只是一般的「區別」。
generatedExample: In the fog, I could barely distinguish the road signs.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思是「他是認真的／他真心那麼想」，不是只是在說說而已。
generatedExample: She said she would help, and she meant it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `delivered over to` 指「被交出去、被移交給」某種對象或命運，帶有被動且無法掌控的意味。
generatedExample: The prisoner was delivered over to the police.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `trickled from` 不是字面「滴落」，而是形容音樂「微弱地流出、斷斷續續地傳出」。屬於帶有感官效果的上下文用法。
generatedExample: Soft piano notes trickled from the open window.

explanationZhTw: `jeering` 原意是「嘲弄的、譏笑的」，用來形容聲音時，表示那個音調帶有輕蔑、刺耳的諷刺感。
generatedExample: The jeering crowd made the speaker lose his composure.

explanationZhTw: `yellow note` 在這裡不是字面上的「黃色音符」，而是一種帶有聯覺或象徵性的說法，讓聲音被感知成某種顏色與情緒。屬於上下文中的特殊意義。
generatedExample: Her voice had a yellow note of bitterness in it.

explanationZhTw: `taking on the semblance of` 表示「開始呈現出……的樣子 / 似乎變成……」，`semblance` 是「外表、表象、近似的樣子」。整個片語強調某物只是看起來像某個狀態。
generatedExample: The shadow was taking on the semblance of a person.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `sold` 不是字面上的「賣出」，而是指「出賣、背叛」。整句在文脈中帶有互相告密或互相背叛的意味。
generatedExample: He felt betrayed when his best friend sold him to the police.

explanationZhTw: 「welled up」在這裡表示情緒或眼淚「湧上來、盈滿」，不是字面上的「井裡湧出」。
generatedExample: Tears welled up in her eyes when she said goodbye.

explanationZhTw: 「waiter」是「侍者、服務生」，屬於日常餐飲場合常見詞彙。
generatedExample: The waiter brought us the menu and two glasses of water.

explanationZhTw: 「came back」是片語動詞，意思是「回來、折返回來」。
generatedExample: She came back with more coffee after a few minutes.

explanationZhTw: 「took up」在這裡是「拿起、舉起」的意思，指把杯子拿到手上。
generatedExample: She took up her glass and smiled.

explanationZhTw: 「sniffed at」是「聞一聞」；這裡表示先把酒杯湊近鼻子聞味道。
generatedExample: He sniffed at the soup before tasting it.

explanationZhTw: 「mouthful」是「一口、滿口」；這裡指每喝一口都更難受。
generatedExample: He needed a mouthful of water after the long run.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻說法，意思是他已經完全沉浸在這種狀態或環境裡，像魚在水中一樣自然。
generatedExample: In that office, gossip was the element he swam in.

explanationZhTw: 「stupor」是「遲鈍、昏迷般的麻木狀態」，常因酒精或疲勞造成。
generatedExample: Too much medicine left him in a stupor for hours.

explanationZhTw: 「revived」是「使恢復精神、喚醒、振作」；這裡指讓他早上重新清醒。
generatedExample: A cold shower revived me after the sleepless night.

explanationZhTw: 「gummed-up」字面是「被黏住的」；這裡形容眼皮因乾黏而難以睜開。
generatedExample: I woke up with gummed-up eyes after crying.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是幽默而誇張的說法，指「從躺著的姿勢起身」。
generatedExample: After the flu, it was hard to rise from the horizontal.

explanationZhTw: 「glazed face」形容臉部表情呆滯、沒有神采，好像蒙上一層霧。
generatedExample: After the long meeting, he stared ahead with a glazed face.

explanationZhTw: 「handy」在這裡是「放在手邊、隨手可拿」的意思。
generatedExample: Keep the charger handy during the trip.

explanationZhTw: 「fixture」指「固定常客、常駐人物」；這裡表示他幾乎天天都在那裡出現。
generatedExample: She became a fixture at the neighborhood bookstore.

explanationZhTw: 「admonished」是「告誡、責備、嚴厲提醒」；這裡是被機器般地警告。
generatedExample: The teacher admonished us not to waste the lab materials.

explanationZhTw: 「sub-committee」是「小組委員會、分委會」，比一般 committee 更小、更下層。
generatedExample: The sub-committee met to review the budget proposal.

explanationZhTw: 「sub-committee」是「小組委員會、分委會」，比一般 committee 更小、更下層。
generatedExample: The sub-committee met to review the budget proposal.

explanationZhTw: 「sprouted」原本是「發芽、長出」；這裡比喻「突然冒出、衍生出來」。
generatedExample: New rules sprouted from the old policy almost overnight.

explanationZhTw: 「compilation」是「編纂、彙編」；這裡指把詞典內容整理成新版。
generatedExample: The compilation of the handbook took several months.

explanationZhTw: 「dispersed」是「解散、散開」；「promptly」表示很快地、立刻地。
generatedExample: The crowd promptly dispersed after the announcement.

explanationZhTw: 「eagerly」是「熱切地、積極地」；這裡帶有諷刺，表示他們表面上很認真。
generatedExample: The students eagerly opened the new assignment.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「minutes」不是「分鐘」，而是「會議紀錄」。
generatedExample: Please send me the minutes from yesterday's meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「drafting」是「起草、草擬」文件。
generatedExample: She spent the afternoon drafting a response to the complaint.

explanationZhTw: 「memoranda」是「備忘錄、正式備忘文件」，是 memorandum 的複數。
generatedExample: The manager circulated several memoranda about the policy change.

explanationZhTw: 「supposedly」是「據說、名義上、表面上」；這裡暗示那個爭論其實未必真有內容。
generatedExample: He was supposedly in charge, but no one listened to him.

explanationZhTw: 「abstruse」表示「艱深難懂的、深奧的」。
generatedExample: The article was so abstruse that I had to read it twice.

explanationZhTw: 「haggling」是「討價還價、爭執細節」；「subtle」表示細微而不明顯的，這裡指在定義上鑽牛角尖。
generatedExample: They spent an hour in subtle haggling over the wording.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「向更高層級請求裁決或支援」的意思，不一定是宗教上的權威。
generatedExample: If they cannot agree, they will appeal to higher authority.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻，意思是他們突然失去精神、活力與興致。
generatedExample: When the music stopped, the energy went out of the room.

explanationZhTw: 「extinct」原意是「滅絕的、熄滅的」；這裡形容眼神毫無生氣，像死掉一樣。
generatedExample: He answered with extinct eyes and no expression.

explanationZhTw: 「cock-crow」是「雞啼、報曉」；這裡借用民間意象，表示天亮時鬼影消失。
generatedExample: The campers woke at cock-crow and started packing.

explanationZhTw: bulletin 指簡短的正式通知或新聞快報；這裡是指螢幕傳來的消息。
generatedExample: The school issued a bulletin about the schedule change.

explanationZhTw: merely 表示「只是、僅僅」，用來降低事情的嚴重性。
generatedExample: I was merely asking a question, not criticizing you.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「在眼皮後面」，而是指他在腦中浮現出地圖的畫面。
generatedExample: After studying all night, the formulas were still behind her eyelids.

explanationZhTw: tearing 在這裡不是「撕裂」，而是形容箭頭代表的軍隊快速、猛烈地向下衝。
generatedExample: The car was tearing down the highway at full speed.

explanationZhTw: reassurance 指「安慰、安心感」；這裡表示他想從那張臉上得到一點安定。
generatedExample: She smiled at me for reassurance before the interview started.

explanationZhTw: imperturbable 表示「鎮定自若的、毫不動搖的」；用來形容那張臉很沉著。
generatedExample: The captain remained imperturbable during the emergency.

explanationZhTw: conceivable 表示「可以想像的、可能成立的」；這裡是在質疑那個想法是否可能。
generatedExample: It is conceivable that the meeting could finish early.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `flagged` 不是指旗幟，而是指「變弱、減退、失去活力」。句意是他的興趣又下降了。
generatedExample: My energy flagged after the long meeting.

explanationZhTw: `mouthful` 指「一口的量」，常用來描述喝下去的一口液體。
generatedExample: She took a mouthful of water before speaking.

explanationZhTw: `picked up` 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「拿起、拎起」。
generatedExample: He picked up the book from the floor.

explanationZhTw: `tentative` 表示「試探性的、不太確定的」。這裡是指這一步棋下得不夠果斷。
generatedExample: She gave a tentative answer to the question.

explanationZhTw: 在西洋棋裡，`Check` 表示「將軍」，也就是國王正受到攻擊。
generatedExample: Check means the king is under threat in chess.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `uncalled` 不是常見的「未被叫到」，而是指「沒有預兆地、突然地」出現。
generatedExample: Uncalled, an old song came back to me while I was walking home.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻用法，意思是「突然浮現在腦海中／想起來」，不是字面上的「漂進去」。
generatedExample: A strange idea floated into her mind during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `counterpaned` 來自 `counterpane`，意思是「床罩／床單被褥」，這裡形容床上鋪著白色床罩。
generatedExample: The old hotel room had a white-counterpaned bed by the window.

explanationZhTw: `dice-box` 是裝骰子的盒子，屬於較具體、偏舊式的名詞。
generatedExample: He picked up the dice-box and set it beside the board.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `disappeared` 不是字面上的「消失」，而是指她不見了、離開了，之後再也看不到。
generatedExample: She disappeared after dinner and never came back.

explanationZhTw: `reconciliation` 指「和解、重修舊好」；這裡是在說他和她之間短暫恢復了親近感。
generatedExample: After the argument, their reconciliation took time.

explanationZhTw: `nagging` 在這裡表示「持續不停、讓人煩惱的」，`nagging hunger` 就是一直揮之不去的飢餓感。
generatedExample: He could not focus because of the nagging headache.

explanationZhTw: 這兩個詞都在形容雨勢很大，`pelting` 和 `drenching` 一起用來強調「大雨傾盆、全身濕透」的感覺。
generatedExample: We got caught in a pelting, drenching storm.

explanationZhTw: `cramped` 表示「狹小擁擠的」，這裡形容臥室空間很窄，讓人不舒服。
generatedExample: They shared a cramped apartment in the city center.

explanationZhTw: `futile` 指「徒勞無功的、沒有用的」；`futile demands` 就是怎麼要求都不會有結果的要求。
generatedExample: His futile demands for extra time were ignored.

explanationZhTw: `sporadically` 表示「偶爾、零星地」；`sporadically open` 就是這家店不是一直營業，只是偶爾開著。
generatedExample: The office was sporadically open during the holidays.

explanationZhTw: `propped up` 是片語動詞，意思是「撐著、靠著支起來」；這裡指小妹妹靠著枕頭坐著。
generatedExample: She sat propped up in bed with a book.

explanationZhTw: 把某件事刻意從腦中趕走，不去想它。
generatedExample: I tried to push the bad news out of my mind and focus on work.

explanationZhTw: 指「假記憶」或「錯誤記憶」：以為自己真的記得，但其實那件事並沒有那樣發生。
generatedExample: Later she realized the scene was a false memory, not an actual event.

explanationZhTw: 被某事困擾、影響；不是單純「有問題」，而是心裡一直在意或不安。
generatedExample: He was troubled by the decision for days.

explanationZhTw: clatter 表示「喀啦一聲的碰撞聲、劈啪作響」，常用來形容硬物掉落或互相碰撞的聲音。
generatedExample: The metal tray fell to the floor with a clatter.

explanationZhTw: as though = 好像、彷彿，用來比喻某件事看起來像另一種情況。
generatedExample: She looked at me as though she knew the answer already.

explanationZhTw: 「shrill」形容聲音尖銳刺耳，常帶有令人不舒服的感覺。
generatedExample: The phone rang with a shrill tone that startled everyone.

explanationZhTw: 「bulletin」指簡短的公告、新聞簡報或正式通報；這裡是指廣播中的重要消息。
generatedExample: We heard a bulletin about the storm on the radio.

explanationZhTw: 「precede」表示「在……之前發生／出現」，這裡是指號角聲先於新聞出現。
generatedExample: A short warning preceded the announcement.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「started」不是單純「開始」，而是指「嚇了一跳、突然一震」。
generatedExample: She started when the door slammed shut.

explanationZhTw: 這個慣用語表示「豎起耳朵仔細聽、立刻注意聽」。
generatedExample: The children pricked up their ears when they heard the word "ice cream".

explanationZhTw: 「let loose」在這裡是「釋放、放出」的意思，表示號角聲突然引發了一大陣噪音。
generatedExample: The fireworks let loose a burst of color over the river.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreen」是這部小說裡的特定裝置名稱，指帶有螢幕與廣播功能的監視/通訊設備，屬於語境中的專有名詞。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the corner announced the evening news.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「drowned」是語境中的特殊用法，意思是「被更大的聲音淹沒、蓋過」，不是指真的溺水。
generatedExample: Her speech was drowned by the noise of the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 「run round」在這裡表示「在各處迅速傳開、流傳」，不是字面上的跑來跑去。
generatedExample: The rumor ran round the school in less than an hour.

explanationZhTw: 「armada」指大規模的艦隊或船隊，這裡特別用來強調軍事力量很龐大。
generatedExample: A small armada of rescue boats headed out before dawn.

explanationZhTw: 「rout」在軍事語境中表示「潰敗、徹底擊潰」，比一般的 defeat 更強烈。
generatedExample: The army turned the battle into a rout before sunset.

explanationZhTw: 「convulsive movements」指不受控制、像抽搐一樣的動作，常用來形容緊張、激動或身體失控的狀態。
generatedExample: She tried to stay calm, but her hands kept making convulsive movements.

explanationZhTw: 「stirred from his seat」在這裡是「離開座位、挪動一下」的意思，不是字面上的攪動。
generatedExample: He did not stir from his seat until the meeting ended.

explanationZhTw: 「cheering himself deaf」是強烈的誇飾說法，表示他在想像中跟著人群大聲歡呼到彷彿自己都聽不見了。
generatedExample: The fans were cheering themselves deaf after the final goal.

explanationZhTw: 「colossus」原意是巨像、巨人；這裡用來比喻極其龐大、壓倒性的存在。
generatedExample: The company became a colossus in the tech industry.

explanationZhTw: 「equivocation」指含糊其詞、閃爍其詞，這裡是說他心裡還有猶豫不定、無法明確判斷的狀態。
generatedExample: Her equivocation made it hard to know what she really wanted.

explanationZhTw: 「perished」表示死亡、毀滅或全然消失；在這裡不只是「死了」，也帶有整體覆滅的語氣。
generatedExample: Many soldiers perished in the battle.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreen」是《1984》裡的特定詞，指那種兼具螢幕與監視/廣播功能的裝置；在這裡不是一般電視，而是帶有控制意味的獨裁科技。
generatedExample: The telescreen crackled to life, and everyone in the room went silent.

explanationZhTw: 「pouring forth」在這裡是文學性的說法，表示聲音或話語源源不絕地湧出，不是字面上真的倒出來。
generatedExample: The announcer's voice poured forth from the speakers all afternoon.

explanationZhTw: 「died down」表示逐漸變小、變安靜，常用來描述噪音、爭吵或風雨慢慢減弱。
generatedExample: The music died down after midnight.

explanationZhTw: 「filled up」這裡是把杯子加滿的意思；屬於日常常見的片語動詞。
generatedExample: She smiled while the waiter filled up her glass.

explanationZhTw: 「white as snow」是慣用語，表示非常潔白；在這裡更是比喻內心毫無罪惡感或污點。
generatedExample: After the apology, he felt white as snow.

explanationZhTw: 「public dock」在這裡不是一般的碼頭，而是法庭上被公開審問、受審的位置，帶有羞辱和政治控制的意味。
generatedExample: The suspect stood in the public dock while the crowd watched in silence.

explanationZhTw: 「implicating」在這裡指供出或牽連別人，使別人也被捲入罪責，而不只是一般的「暗示」。
generatedExample: His testimony ended up implicating everybody in the scheme.

explanationZhTw: 這是強烈的文學性與反諷用法；「long-hoped-for bullet」不是字面上真的期待子彈，而是指終於到來的死亡。
generatedExample: For the prisoner, the long-hoped-for bullet meant the end of all suffering.

explanationZhTw: 非常大的；這裡用來強調那張臉巨大而有壓迫感。
generatedExample: The enormous building dominated the skyline.

explanationZhTw: 任性、自作主張；指一個人固執地照自己的意思行事。
generatedExample: He was self-willed and refused every suggestion.

explanationZhTw: 文學用法，指溫暖的懷抱、母性的庇護或養育之所，不是字面上的胸部。
generatedExample: After the long trip, the child ran back to his mother's loving breast.

explanationZhTw: 帶有琴酒氣味的；這裡是帶酒味的文學意象，暗示醉態和頹敗。
generatedExample: The room was filled with gin-scented air after the celebration.

explanationZhTw: 緩慢地往下流。
generatedExample: A thin stream of rain trickled down the window.

explanationZhTw: 沒事了、好了；在這裡表示一切終於安定下來。
generatedExample: Don't worry, everything is all right now.

explanationZhTw: 沒事了、好了；在這裡表示一切終於安定下來。
generatedExample: Don't worry, everything is all right now.

explanationZhTw: 掙扎、奮鬥；這裡指內心或人生中的痛苦鬥爭。
generatedExample: She went through a long struggle before she succeeded.

explanationZhTw: 勝利；這裡是比喻用法，指他在內心鬥爭中贏了自己。
generatedExample: Their victory came after months of hard training.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的獨裁象徵人物；在小說語境中是政治權威與監控的象徵。
generatedExample: In the novel, Big Brother represents total control.
